correctness.justification: Every claim verified against the code: ReportView only POSTs, PostPage is at line 17 (~L20 approximate), /api/incr path, nav singular, gutter prop, mod abbreviation, buf, iso, active, p, Props, rows/value all exist as described.

coverage.justification: Catches the high-impact misleading names (ReportView, PostPage), public URL jargon, convention violations, abbreviations, and boolean prefix; misses a few minor parallels like `s` in socials.map and the file-name mismatch between view.tsx and ReportView.

prioritization.justification: Critical bucket correctly captures misleading/lying names and the externally-visible URL; high/medium/low gradient is defensible and maps to blast radius.

actionability.justification: Each item names a specific target (ReportView→ViewTracker, gutter→aside, buf→digest, etc.) with an exact file location, safe for mechanical application.

rationale.justification: Every finding includes a crisp why — domain mismatch, convention precedent, self-describing inputs, greppability — not just assertion.

signal_density.justification: Tight two-line entries, no boilerplate or filler; each line carries information.

overall_notes: Accurate, well-prioritized audit with concrete replacements and clean why-it-matters explanations. Strongest on signal density and actionability; coverage is solid but leaves a few minor parallels unmentioned. Prioritization of misleading-name issues (ReportView, PostPage) as critical is well-reasoned.
